title: 'Rocketman' Sings, but Will It Soar? I Exit Survey
format: Sean and Amanda review the Elton John biopic 'Rocketman,' then survey the rest of the weekend's new releases, including the Netflix rom-com 'Always Be My Maybe.'

segments.heading: 'Rocketman'
segments.note: the Elton John biopic, reviewed against 'Bohemian Rhapsody'

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: Sean went in skeptical of music biopics, of Taron Egerton singing Elton John's songs, and of director Dexter Fletcher, who took over 'Bohemian Rhapsody' after Bryan Singer left, but came out won over. Both praise its fantastical staging, Jamie Bell's Bernie Taupin, and the Elton-Bernie partnership as the real love story; both are less sold on the childhood scenes and the pat 'his parents didn't love him' framing. Amanda liked it more than 'Bohemian Rhapsody' but wonders if it's 'not dumb enough' to be a crowd-pleaser.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: The film Sean and Amanda keep measuring 'Rocketman' against. They agree 'Rocketman' is more inventive and more candid about its subject's addiction and sexuality, but less of a pure singalong pleasure, and unlikely to match this film's box office or awards run.

segments.heading: Exit Survey
segments.note: the rest of the weekend's new releases

films.title: Godzilla: King of the Monsters
films.note: Rocketman's counterprogramming target. Sean saw it and says it 'has a lot of flaws.'

films.title: Ma
films.note: Tate Taylor's Blumhouse horror movie. Sean: 'I can't say that I would recommend it.'

films.title: Always Be My Maybe
films.note: Nahnatchka Khan's Netflix rom-com starring Ali Wong and Randall Park, born from an Ali Wong New Yorker profile quote. Amanda calls it 'a sitcom,' formulaic and underwritten for its leads, but says a long, self-lampooning Keanu Reeves cameo saves it and is the part people will remember. Both recommend it as an easy watch-at-home pick.
